ophthalmicNerve
---------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/ophthalmicNerve
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nerve of head region. Is part of the trigeminal nerve. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000348) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The sensory nerve subdivision of the trigeminal nerve that transmits sensory information from the orbit and its contents, the nasal cavity and the skin of the nose and forehead. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000348)]
   :name: ophthalmic nerve
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0000348#ophthalmic-nerve
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000348

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

ophthalmicPlexus
----------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/ophthalmicPlexus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nerve plexus. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011194)]
   :description: An autonomic plexus, entering the orbit in company with the ophthalmic artery, derived from the internal carotid plexus. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011194)]
   :name: ophthalmic plexus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0011194#ophthalmic-plexus
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011194

opticChiasma
------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/opticChiasma
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a decussation of diencephalon. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000959)]
   :description: A decussation of the diencephalon where the fibers of the optic nerve cross. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000959)]
   :name: optic chiasma
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0000959#optic-chiasm
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000959

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

opticDisc
---------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/opticDisc
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is part of the retina. [auto-generated from 'relationship' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001783)]
   :description: The optic disc or optic nerve head is the location where ganglion cell axons exit the eye to form the optic nerve. There are no light sensitive rods or cones to respond to a light stimulus at this point. This causes a break in the visual field called 'the blind spot' or the 'physiological blind spot'. The optic nerve head in a normal human eye carries from 1 to 1.2 million neurons from the eye towards the brain. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001783)]
   :name: optic disc
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001783#optic-disc
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001783

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

opticRadiation
--------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/opticRadiation
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a radiation of cerebral hemisphere. Is part of the forebrain ipsilateral fiber tracts. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022264) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The optic radiation (also known as the geniculo-calcarine tract or as the geniculostriate pathway) is a collection of axons from relay neurons in the lateral geniculate nucleus of the thalamus carrying visual information to the visual cortex (also called striate cortex) along the calcarine fissure. There is one such tract on each side of the brain. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022264)]
   :name: optic radiation
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0022264#optic-radiation
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022264

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

---------------------------

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/opticRecessOfThirdVentricle
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is an anatomical entity. Is part of the third ventricle. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002287) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Recess in third ventricle lying in front of the optic chiasm at the base of the lamina terminalis. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002287)]
   :name: optic recess of third ventricle
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002287#optic-recess-of-third-ventricle-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002287

opticStalk
----------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/opticStalk
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a developing neuroepithelium. Is part of the diencephalon. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0003098) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The narrow, proximal portion of the optic vesicle which connects the embryonic eye and forebrain. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0003098)]
   :name: optic stalk
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0003098#optic-stalk
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0003098

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

opticTract
----------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/opticTract
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a tract of diencephalon. Is part of the diencephalic white matter. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001908) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Diencephalic white matter (tract) which is comprised of retinal ganglion cell axons after which they have passed through the optic chiasm. Predominantly white matter structure found in diencephalon consisting of fibers originating in the retina. The optic tract is considered to extend from the point of the optic chiasm and terminates largely, although not exclusively, in the lateral geniculate complex. Other fibers end in the superior colliculus and other structures in the diencephalon, midbrain and brainstem (MM). [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001908)]
   :name: optic tract
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001908#optic-tract-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001908

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

oralPartOfSpinalTrigeminalNucleus
---------------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/oralPartOfSpinalTrigeminalNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a gray matter of hindbrain. Is part of the spinal nucleus of trigeminal nerve. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002591) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :name: oral part of spinal trigeminal nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002591#oral-part-of-spinal-trigeminal-nucleus-1

------------

oralPontineReticularNucleus
---------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/oralPontineReticularNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nucleus of pontine reticular formation. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002999)]
   :description: The oral pontine reticular nucleus is delineated from its caudal brother, with which it shares its first three names. This nucleus tapers into the lower mesencephalic reticular formation and contains sporadic giant cells. Different populations of the pontis oralis have displayed discharge patterns which coordinate with phasic movements to and from paradoxical sleep. From this information it has been implied that the n.r. pontis oralis is involved in the mediation of changing to and from REM sleep. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002999)]
   :name: oral pontine reticular nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002999#oral-pontine-reticular-nucleus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002999

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

orbitalGyrus
------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/orbitalGyrus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a gyrus. Is part of the frontal lobe. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0007193) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The inferior or orbital surface of the frontal lobe is concave, and rests on the orbital plate of the frontal bone. It is divided into four orbital gyri by a well-marked H-shaped orbital sulcus. These are named, from their position, the medial, anterior, lateral, and posterior orbital gyri. The medial orbital gyrus presents a well-marked antero-posterior sulcus, the olfactory sulcus, for the olfactory tract; the portion medial to this is named the straight gyrus, and is continuous with the superior frontal gyrus on the medial surface. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0007193)]
   :name: orbital gyrus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0007193#orbital-gyrus
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0007193

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

outerLimitingLayerOfRetina
--------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/outerLimitingLayerOfRetina
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a limiting membrane of retina. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001788)]
   :description: The row of jucntional complexes between the plasma membranes of rod segments and the Muller glia cells; this barrier separates the layer of inner and outer segments of the rods and cones from the outer nuclear layer and forms a blood-retina barrier. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001788)]
   :name: outer limiting layer of retina
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001788#outer-limiting-layer-of-retina
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001788

   :description: The layer within the retina where the photoreceptor cell bodies reside. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001789)]

outerPlexiformLayerOfRetina
---------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>

   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure

   :description: The layer within the retina where the bipolar cells synapse with the photoreceptor cells. Between the inner and outer nuclear layers, the outer plexiform layer (OPL) contains connections between the photoreceptors and bipolar and horizontal cells. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001790)]
   :name: outer plexiform layer of retina
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001790#outer-plexiform-layer-of-retina

------------

paleocortex
-----------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/paleocortex
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is an allocortex. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014735)]
   :description: Part of the allocortex defined on the basis of cytoarchitecture. The other two are archicortex and periallocortex. It consists of very thin, primitive cortex with few clearly defined layers. It is distinguished ontogenetically by the fact that it does not develop through a cortical plate. It includes the olfactory bulb, accessory olfactory bulb, olfactory tubercle, septum, prepyriform area and periamygdalar area ( Stephan-1975 ). (adapted from Brain Info) [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014735)]
   :name: paleocortex
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0014735#paleocortex
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014735

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

pallium

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/pallium
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a regional part of brain. Is part of the telencephalon. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000203) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Dorsal part (roof region) of the telencephalon. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000203)]
   :name: pallium
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0000203#pallium
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000203

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

palmarBranchOfMedianNerve
-------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/palmarBranchOfMedianNerve
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is an arm nerve. Is part of the median nerve. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0016430) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The palmar branch of the median nerve is a branch of the median nerve which arises at the lower part of the forearm and innervates skin of proximal central palm and thenar eminence. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0016430)]
   :name: palmar branch of median nerve
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0016430#palmar-branch-of-median-nerve
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0016430

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

palpebralBranchOfInfraOrbitalNerve
----------------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/palpebralBranchOfInfraOrbitalNerve
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is an eyelid nerve and nerve of head region. Is part of the infra-orbital nerve. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022297) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A nerve that innervates an eyelid and is a branch of the infra-orbital branch of the maxillary nerve. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022297)]
   :name: palpebral branch of infra-orbital nerve
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0022297#palpebral-branch-of-infra-orbital-nerve
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022297

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

parabigeminalNucleus
--------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/parabigeminalNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nucleus of midbrain tectum. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002140)]
   :description: Cholinergic cell group that is located ventral to the brachium of the inferior colliculus (in rat) and has reciprocal connectivity with the superior colliculus (Adapted from Paxinos, The rat central nervous system, 2nd ed, 1995, pg 865. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002140)]
   :name: parabigeminal nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002140#parabigeminal-nucleus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002140

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

parabrachialNucleus
-------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/parabrachialNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a brainstem nucleus and hindbrain nucleus. Is part of the pons. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0007634) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A nucleus of the parabrachial area, which in the human brain is a horseshoe-shaped strip of gray matter comprising the Kölliker-Fuse nucleus, the lateral parabrachial nucleus and the medial parabrachial nucleus. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0007634)]
   :name: parabrachial nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0007634#parabrachial-nucleus
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0007634

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

parabrachialPigmentalNucleus
----------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/parabrachialPigmentalNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a brainstem nucleus, nucleus of midbrain tegmentum and midbrain nucleus. Is part of the ventral tegmental area. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013738) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Nucleus identified in the medial ventral tegmental area that borders the red nucleus and superior cerebellar peduncle dorsally, the medial lemniscus laterally, the subtantia nigra pars compacta and paranigral nucleus ventrally and the rostral linear nucleus and interfascicular nucleus medially. Generally, the largest nucleus in the ventral tegmental area, occupying an average of 50% of the total volume as measured in rat, cat, monkey and human. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013738)]
   :name: parabrachial pigmental nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0013738#parabrachial-pigmented-nucleus
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013738

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

----------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>

   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a gyrus. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0007190)]
   :name: paracentral gyrus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0007190#paracentral-gyrus
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0007190

------------

paracentralLobule
-----------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/paracentralLobule
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a gyrus. Is part of the cerebral cortex. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0035933) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The junction of the precentral gyrus and postcentral gyrus on the medial surface of the cerebral cortex. It lies across the boundary between the frontal lobe and the parietal lobe. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0035933)]
   :name: paracentral lobule
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0035933#paracentral-lobule
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0035933

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

paracentralNucleus
------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/paracentralNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nucleus of dorsal thalamus. Is part of the rostral intralaminar nuclear group. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001924) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: One of the intralaminar nuclei of the thalamus, located medial to the central lateral nucleus. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001924)]
   :name: paracentral nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001924#paracentral-nucleus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001924

   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a sulcus of brain. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002598)]
   :description: The paracentral sulcus is a sulcus of the brain. It forms the paracentral lobule's anterior border. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002598)]
   :name: paracentral sulcus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002598#paracentral-sulcus-1

parahippocampalGyrus
--------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/parahippocampalGyrus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a gyrus. Is part of the limbic system, the temporal lobe and the limbic lobe. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002973) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Component of the temporal lobe on the mesial surface, posterior to the entorhinal cortex. The rostral and caudal boundaries are the posterior end of the netorhinal cortex and the caudal portion of the hippocampus, respectively. The medial boundary is designated as the medial aspect off the temporal lobe and the lateral boundary is the collateral sulcus (Christine Fennema-Notestine). [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002973)]
   :name: parahippocampal gyrus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002973#parahippocampal-gyrus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002973

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

paramedianLobule

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/paramedianLobule
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a cerebellum vermis lobule. Is part of the cerebellum posterior vermis. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0005349) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The anterior portion of the posteroinferior lobule of the cerebellum, the posterior portion being the semilunar lobule inferior; the two are continuous with the tuber of the vermis. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0005349)]
   :name: paramedian lobule
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0005349#paramedian-lobule

   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/parasubiculum
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a regional part of brain. Is part of the hippocampal formation. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004683) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A transitional zone between the presubiculum and the entorhinal area in the mouse (Paxinos-2001), the rat (Swanson-1998) and the primate (Zilles-1990). Defined on the basis of cytoarchitecture, it is more similar to the presubiculum than to the entorhinal area (Zilles-1990). (from Brain Info) [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004683)]
   :name: parasubiculum
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0004683#parasubiculum-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004683

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

parasympatheticGanglion
-----------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/parasympatheticGanglion
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is an autonomic ganglion. Is part of the parasympathetic nervous system. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001808) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Ganglion containing neurons that receive innervation from parasympathetic neurons in the central nervous system and subserves parasympathetic functions through innervation of smooth muscle, cardiac muscle and glands. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001808)]
   :name: parasympathetic ganglion
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001808#parasympathetic-ganglion
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001808

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

   :description: A nerve that is part of a parasympathetic nervous system. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004293)]
   :name: parasympathetic nerve
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0004293#parasympathetic-nerve
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004293

paratenialNucleus
-----------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/paratenialNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nucleus of dorsal thalamus. Is part of the midline nuclear group. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002992) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: One of the median nuclei of the thalamus, situated ventral and medial to the stria medullaris. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002992)]
   :name: paratenial nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002992#paratenial-nucleus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002992

------------

paraterminalGyrus
-----------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/paraterminalGyrus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a supracallosal gyrus. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002603)]
   :description: The paraterminal gyrus (subcallosal gyrus, peduncle of the corpus callosum) is a narrow lamina on the medial surface of the hemisphere in front of the lamina terminalis, behind the parolfactory area, and below the rostrum of the corpus callosum. It is continuous around the genu of the corpus callosum with the supracallosal gyrus. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002603)]
   :name: paraterminal gyrus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002603#paraterminal-gyrus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002603

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

paratrigeminalNucleus
---------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/paratrigeminalNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nucleus of medulla oblongata. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0016832)]
   :description: The term paratrigeminal nucleus refers to a group of cells located lateral to the spinal trigeminal tract of the medulla and medial to the spinocerebellar tracts at the level of the spinal trigeminal nucleus. The cells, identified by Nissl stain, are partially embedded in the adjacent tracts; their distribution varies by species. The paratrigeminal nucleus may represent an extension of the lateral cervical nucleus into the medulla ( Swanson-2004 ). It is found in the human ( Carpenter-1983 ), the macaque ( Paxinos-2009a ), the rat ( Swanson-2004 ) and the mouse ( Paxinos-2001 ). In the functional model of central nervous system organization it is classified as part of the subcortical somatosensory system ( Swanson-2004 ) [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0016832)]
   :name: paratrigeminal nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0016832#paratrigeminal-nucleus
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0016832

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

paraventricularNucleusOfHypothalamus
------------------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/paraventricularNucleusOfHypothalamus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a hypothalamic nucleus. Is part of the periventricular zone of hypothalamus. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001930) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Nucleus in the anterior part of the hypothalamus. (MSH) * one of the magnocellular hypothalamic nuclei, an elongated plate of large, deeply staining cells located close to the third ventricle in the anterior hypothalamic area; major source of oxytocin and to a lesser extent, of antidiuretic hormone, neurohormones, which are carried to the neurohypophysis along the paraventriculohypophysial tract. (CSP) [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001930)]
   :name: paraventricular nucleus of hypothalamus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001930#paraventricular-nucleus-of-hypothalamus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001930

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

paraventricularNucleusOfThalamus

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/paraventricularNucleusOfThalamus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nucleus of dorsal thalamus. Is part of the midline nuclear group. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001920) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The nucleus paraventricularis anterior thalami and nuclues paraventricularis posterior thalami, two of the nuclei mediani thalami; they are situated on the dorsomedial wall of the thalamus, juxtaposed to the third ventricle. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001920)]
   :name: paraventricular nucleus of thalamus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001920#paraventricular-nucleus-of-thalamus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001920

parietalCortex
--------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/parietalCortex
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a cortex of cerebral lobe. Is part of the parietal lobe. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0016530) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Gray matter of the parietal region of the neocortex, located in the parietal lobe of gyrencephalic animals. It is continuous anteriorly with the frontal cortex, posteriorly with the occipital cortex and medially with the insular cortex and with the temporal cortex on the posterior/inferior border. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0016530)]
   :name: parietal cortex
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0016530#parietal-cortex
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0016530

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

parietalOperculum
-----------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/parietalOperculum
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a regional part of brain. Is part of the operculum of brain. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002911) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The parietal operculum, forming the superior bank of the sylvian fissure, as studied in the cat, contains the secondary somatosensory representation, 'S-II', and a second somatotopic representation (parietal ventral, or PV). Anatomically, primate S-II receives inputs from area 3 and area 1, and projects to PV and area 7. PV has projections to area 5 and premotor areas. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002911)]
   :name: parietal operculum
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002911#parietal-operculum-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002911

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

parietalOrgan
-------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/parietalOrgan
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is part of the pineal complex. [auto-generated from 'relationship' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004869)]
   :description: A part of the epithalamus present in some animal species. The eye may be photoreceptive and is usually associated with the pineal gland, regulating circadian rhythmicity and hormone production for thermoregulation. The parietal eye is a part of the epithalamus, which can be divided into two major parts; the epiphysis (the pineal organ, or pineal gland if mostly endocrine) and the parietal organ (often called the parietal eye, or third eye if it is photoreceptive). It arises as an anterior evagination of the pineal organ or as a separate outgrowth of the roof of the diencephalon. In some species, it protrudes through the skull. The parietal eye uses a different biochemical method of detecting light than rod cells or cone cells in a normal vertebrate eye. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004869)]
   :name: parietal organ
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0004869#parietal-organ
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004869

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

parietoOccipitalSulcus
----------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/parietoOccipitalSulcus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a subarachnoid sulcus. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002695)]
   :description: Only a small part of the Parietooccipital Fissure (or parieto-occipital sulcus) is seen on the lateral surface of the hemisphere, its chief part being on the medial surface. The lateral part of the parietooccipital fissure (Fig. 726) is situated about 5 cm. in front of the occipital pole of the hemisphere, and measures about 1.25 cm. in length. The medial part of the parietooccipital fissure (Fig. 727) runs downward and forward as a deep cleft on the medial surface of the hemisphere, and joins the calcarine fissure below and behind the posterior end of the corpus callosum. In most cases it contains a submerged gyrus. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002695)]
   :name: parieto-occipital sulcus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002695#parieto-occipital-sulcus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002695

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>

   :description: A neurosecretory center of the insect brain, located along the anterior midline.    :definition: Is a gray matter of diencephalon. Is part of the medial dorsal nucleus of thalamus and the medial dorsal nucleus of thalamus. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002654) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :name: parvicellular part of medial dorsal nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002654#parvicellular-part-of-medial-dorsal-nucleus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002654

   :name: parvocellular layer of dorsal nucleus of lateral geniculate body

------------

parvocellularOculomotorNucleus
------------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/parvocellularOculomotorNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a brainstem nucleus and midbrain nucleus. Is part of the midbrain tegmentum. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002141) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Nucleus that is part of the oculomotor nerve complex consisting of preganglionic parasympathetic neurons situated close to the midline at the level of the superior colliculus (Heimer, the Human Brain and Spinal Cord, 1994, pg 244) [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002141)]
   :name: parvocellular oculomotor nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002141#parvocellular-oculomotor-nucleus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002141

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

parvocellularPartOfRedNucleus
-----------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/parvocellularPartOfRedNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a gray matter of midbrain. Is part of the red nucleus. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002938) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The parvocellular red nucleus (RNp) is located in the rostral midbrain and is involved in motor coordination. Together with the magnocellular red nucleus, it makes up the red nucleus. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002938)]
   :name: parvocellular part of red nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002938#parvocellular-part-of-red-nucleus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002938

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a diencephalic nucleus. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2002219)]
   :name: parvocellular preoptic nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:2002219#parvocellular-preoptic-nucleus
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2002219

------------

parvocellularReticularNucleus
-----------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/parvocellularReticularNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a lateral reticular nucleus. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0016633)]
   :description: The parvocellular reticular nucleus is part of the brain located dorsolateral to the nucleus reticularis pontis caudalis. The dorsal portion of the reticular nucleus has been shown to innervate the mesencephalic trigeminal nucleus and its surrounding area. Also, it projects to the facial nucleus, hypoglossal nucleus and parabrachial nucleus along with parts of the caudal parvocellular reticular formation. This nucleus is also involved in expiration with a part of the gigantocellular nucleus. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0016633)]
   :name: parvocellular reticular nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0016633#parvocellular-reticular-nucleus
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0016633

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a diencephalic nucleus and superficial pretectal nucleus. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0035564)]
   :description: A nucleus that is part of the superficial pretectum and is characterized by small cells. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0035564)]
   :name: parvocellular superficial pretectal nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0035564#parvocellular-superficial-pretectal-nucleus

------------

pedunculopontineTegmentalNucleus
--------------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/pedunculopontineTegmentalNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nucleus of midbrain reticular formation. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002142)]
   :description: The pedunculopontine nucleus (PPN) (or pedunculopontine tegmental nucleus, PPTN) is located in the brainstem, caudal to the substantia nigra and adjacent to the superior cerebellar peduncle. It is composed by a wide variety of neurochemical cell types, including cholinergic, glutamatergic and GABAergic cells. In the classical sense, the PPN is considered to be one of the main components of the reticular activating system. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002142)]
   :name: pedunculopontine tegmental nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002142#pedunculopontine-tegmental-nucleus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002142

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

perforantPath
-------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/perforantPath
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a tract of brain. Is part of the white matter of telencephalon and the forebrain ipsilateral fiber tracts. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034931) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A pathway of fibers originating in the lateral part of the entorhinal area, perforating the subiculum of the hippocampus, and running into the stratum moleculare of the hippocampus, where these fibers synapse with others that go to the dentate gyrus. (Dorland, 28th ed) [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034931)]
   :name: perforant path
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0034931#perforant-path
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034931

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

periamygdaloidCortex
--------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/periamygdaloidCortex
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a regional part of brain. Is part of the cerebral cortex and the corticomedial nuclear complex. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014537) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Is located ventral to the basal nucleus and has three subdivisions: the periamygdaloid cortex, the medial division, and the sulcal division. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014537)]
   :name: periamygdaloid cortex
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0014537#periamygdaloid-cortex-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014537

------------

pericalcarineCortex
-------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/pericalcarineCortex
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a regional part of brain. Is part of the parietal lobe. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022534) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Component of the parietal lobe. The rostral boundary of the pericalcarine cortex was the first appearance of the calcarine sulcus whereas the caudal boundary was the most posterior coronal slice where the calcarine sulcus was visualized. The medial and lateral boundaries were the medial portion of the temporal and occipital cortices and the inferomedial end of the calcarine sulcus respectively (Christine Fennema-Notestine). [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022534)]
   :name: pericalcarine cortex
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0022534#pericalcarine-cortex-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022534

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

   :definition: Is a hypothalamic nucleus. Is part of the lateral zone of hypothalamus. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002274) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :name: perifornical nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002274#perifornical-nucleus
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002274

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

perifovealPartOfRetina
----------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/perifovealPartOfRetina
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is part of the macula lutea. [auto-generated from 'relationship' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0018105)]
   :description: The outermost region surrounding the fovea centralis. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0018105)]
   :name: perifoveal part of retina
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0018105#perifoveal-part-of-retina
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0018105

perinealNerve
-------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/perinealNerve
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a pelvis nerve. Is part of the pudendal nerve. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011391) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A nerve arising from the pudendal nerve that supplies the perineum. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011391)]
   :name: perineal nerve
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0011391#perineal-nerve
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011391

perineurium
-----------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/perineurium
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is an anatomical entity. Is part of the nerve fasciculus. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000121) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A layer of thin, concentrically arranged cells with interspersed collagen that lies within the epineurium. (Nolte, J., The Human Brain 3rd edition) [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000121)]
   :name: perineurium
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0000121#perineurium
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000121

periolivaryNucleus
------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/periolivaryNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nucleus of superior olivary complex. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002971)]
   :description: A nucleus of the superior olivary complex that surrounds the primary superior olivary nuclei. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002971)]
   :name: periolivary nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002971#periolivary-nucleus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002971

periostealDuraMater
-------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/periostealDuraMater
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a layer of dura mater. Is part of the brain dura mater. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0010505) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The outermost of the two layers of the cranial dura mater which normally always adheres to the periosteum of the bones of the cranial vault. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0010505)]
   :name: periosteal dura mater
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0010505#periosteal-dura-mater
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0010505

------------

peripeduncularNucleus
---------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/peripeduncularNucleus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a brainstem nucleus, nucleus of midbrain tegmentum and midbrain nucleus. Is part of the midbrain tegmentum. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002144) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A group of neuron cell bodies that form a thin, caplike configuration over the dorsolateral aspect of the crus cerebri; many of its cells are acetylcholinesterase positive. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002144)]
   :name: peripeduncular nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002144#peripeduncular-nucleus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002144

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

peripheralRegionOfRetina
------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/peripheralRegionOfRetina
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a regional part of nervous system. Is part of the retina. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013682) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Region of retina that extends from central retina to ora serrata. Peripheral retina is dominated by rods and also has less ganglion cells and is not as thick as central retina. It is responsible for peripheral and night vision. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013682)]
   :name: peripheral region of retina
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0013682#peripheral-region-of-retina
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013682

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

perirhinalCortex
----------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/perirhinalCortex
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a regional part of brain. Is part of the cerebral cortex. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0006083) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Region of cortex near to the rhinal sulcus. It is defined differently in different nomenclatures, due in part to the lack of agreement on the definition of the border of this region. In the rat, the perirhinal cortex occupies only caudal levels of the rhinal sulcus while in the monkey, it occurpies the full rostrocaudal extent of the rhinal sulcus (Burwell et al., Hippocampus, 5: 390-408, 1995). [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0006083)]
   :name: perirhinal cortex
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0006083#perirhinal-cortex
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0006083

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

pharyngealBranchOfVagusNerve

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/pharyngealBranchOfVagusNerve
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nerve of head region. Is part of the vagus nerve. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000929) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Motor nerve of the pharynx, arises from the upper part of the ganglion nodosum, and consists principally of filaments from the cranial portion of the accessory nerve. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000929)]
   :name: pharyngeal branch of vagus nerve
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0000929#pharyngeal-branch-of-vagus-nerve

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/pharyngealNervePlexus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is an autonomic nerve plexus. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011325)]
   :description: The pharyngeal plexus is a network of nerve fibers innervating most of the palate, larynx, and pharynx. It is located on the surface of the middle pharyngeal constrictor muscle. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011325)]
   :name: pharyngeal nerve plexus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0011325#pharyngeal-nerve-plexus
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011325

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/photoreceptorLayerOfRetina
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a layer of retina. Is part of the retinal neural layer. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001787) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The layer within the retina where the photoreceptor cell receptor segments reside. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001787)]
   :name: photoreceptor layer of retina
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001787#photoreceptor-layer-of-retina
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001787

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

photoreceptorOuterSegmentLayer
------------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/photoreceptorOuterSegmentLayer
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a layer of retina. Is part of the photoreceptor layer of retina. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0003926) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :name: photoreceptor outer segment layer
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0003926#photoreceptor-outer-segment-layer
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0003926

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

phrenicNerve
------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/phrenicNerve
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a thoracic cavity nerve. Is part of the nerve of cervical vertebra. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001884) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A nerve that arises from the caudal cervical nerves and is primarily the motor nerve of the diaphragm but also sends sensory fibers to the pericardium. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001884)]
   :name: phrenic nerve
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001884#phrenic-nerve
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001884

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

piaMater
--------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/piaMater
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a leptomeninx. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002361)]
   :description: The innermost layer of the leptomeninges, consisting of a delicate membrane closely covering the surface of the brain and spinal cord,and lying under the arachnoid membrane. The pia, unlike the arachnoid, extends into the sulci in gyrencephalic animals. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002361)]
   :name: pia mater
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002361#pia-mater
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002361

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

pigmentedLayerOfRetina
----------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/pigmentedLayerOfRetina
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a layer of retina. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001782)]
   :description: A monolayer of pigmented epithelium covering the neural retina; develops from the outer of the two layers of the optic cup. the pigmented cell layer just outside the neurosensory retina that nourishes retinal visual cells, and is firmly attached to the underlying choroid and overlying retinal visual cells. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001782)]
   :name: pigmented layer of retina
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001782#pigmented-layer-of-retina
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001782

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

pinealBody
----------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/pinealBody
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a gland of diencephalon and secretory circumventricular organ. Is part of the pineal complex. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001905) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A midline, cone like structure located in the dorso-caudal roof of the 3rd ventricle, attached by peduncles to the habenular and posterior commissures. The stalk contains nerve fibers, blood vessels, connective tissue and parenchymal cells (Paxinos, The Rat Central Nervous System, 2nd ed, pg 399). [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001905)]
   :name: pineal body
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001905#pineal-body-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001905

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

pinealComplex
-------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/pinealComplex
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is an anatomical entity. Is part of the epithalamus. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0015238) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A cluster in the epithalamus that consists of the pineal body and any associated structures, such as the parapineal gland or the parietal organ. The complex is poorly developed in mammals. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0015238)]
   :name: pineal complex
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0015238#pineal-complex
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0015238

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

pinealCorporaArenacea
---------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/pinealCorporaArenacea
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a calcified structure of brain. Is part of the pineal body. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0017632) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A calcified structure of brain that is part of a pineal body. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0017632)]
   :name: pineal corpora arenacea
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0017632#pineal-corpora-arenacea
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0017632

pinealGlandStalk
----------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/pinealGlandStalk
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a regional part of brain. Is part of the pineal body. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011768) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :name: pineal gland stalk
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0011768#pineal-gland-stalk
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011768

pinealParenchyma
----------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/pinealParenchyma
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a parenchyma of central nervous system. Is part of the pineal body. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034907) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Parenchymal tissue that forms the pineal gland. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034907)]
   :name: pineal parenchyma
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0034907#pineal-parenchyma
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034907

pinealRecessOfThirdVentricle
----------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/pinealRecessOfThirdVentricle
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is an anatomical entity. Is part of the third ventricle. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022283) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The diverticulum of the thin roof of the dorsocaudal third ventricle that projects into the stalk of the pineal gland. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022283)]
   :name: pineal recess of third ventricle
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0022283#pineal-recess-of-third-ventricle
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022283
