------------

ventralRootOfSpinalCord
-----------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/ventralRootOfSpinalCord
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a spinal nerve root. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002260)]
   :description: The ventral roots contain efferent motor axons. Similar to the dorsal roots, the ventral roots continue out from the spinal column, and meet and mix with their corresponding dorsal nerve root at a point after the ganglion. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002260)]
   :name: ventral root of spinal cord
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002260#ventral-root-of-spinal-cord
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002260

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

ventralStriatum
---------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/ventralStriatum
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a gray matter of telencephalon. Is part of the striatum. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0005403) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A composite structure of the telencephalon that is defined in the striatopallidal system by connectivity and neurochemical staining. It includes the nucleus accumbens, the most ventral portions of the caudate nucleus and the putamen, the rostrolateral portion of the anterior perforated substance, the islands of Calleja and a rostral subcommissural portion of substantia innominata. The boundary between the ventral striatum and dorsal striatum is indistinct in sections stained for Nissl substance (Heimer-1995; adapted from Brain Info). [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0005403)]
   :name: ventral striatum
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0005403#ventral-striatum
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0005403

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure

   :name: ventral sulcus

------------

ventralSupraopticDecussation
----------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/ventralSupraopticDecussation
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a commissure of diencephalon. Is part of the anterior hypothalamic region. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002676) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The ventral supraoptic decussation is the crossover point for signals from the left and right eye, en route respectively to the right and left sides of the visual cortex. Occupying the posterior part of the commissure of the optic chiasma is a strand of fibers, the Ventral supraoptic decussation (commissure of Gudden, Gudden's inferior commissure), which is not derived from the optic nerves; it forms a connecting link between the medial geniculate bodies. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002676)]
   :name: ventral supraoptic decussation
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002676#ventral-supraoptic-decussation-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002676

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a sympathetic trunk. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_3010771)]
   :name: ventral sympathetic chain
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:3010771#ventral-sympathetic-chain

------------

ventralTegmentalArea
--------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/ventralTegmentalArea
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a regional part of brain. Is part of the midbrain tegmentum. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002691) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Unpaired midbrain region situated in the ventromedial portion of the reticular formation. The VTA is medial to the substantia nigra and ventral to the red nucleus, and extends caudally from the posterior hypothalamus in the diencephalon. The VTA contains dopamine neurons that project to various limbic and cortical areas and is a critical component of the brain's reward circuitry. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002691)]
   :name: ventral tegmental area
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002691#ventral-tegmental-area-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002691

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a neural decussation. Is part of the ventral tegmental area. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002615) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]

   :description: A nucleus of brain that is part of a ventral tegmental area. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002438)]
   :name: ventral tegmental nucleus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002438#ventral-tegmental-nucleus
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002438

------------

ventralThalamicFasciculus
-------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/ventralThalamicFasciculus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a fasciculus of brain. Is part of the ventral thalamus and the forebrain ipsilateral fiber tracts. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022254) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The thalamic fasciculus is a component of the subthalamus. It is sometimes considered synonymous with 'field H1 of Forel'. Nerve fibres forming a composite bundle containing cerebellothalamic (crossed) and pallidothalamic (uncrossed) fibres that is insinuated between the thalamus and zona incerta. The thalamic fasciculus consists of the joint fibers of the ansa lenticularis and the lenticular fasciculus, coming from different portions of the medial globus pallidus, before they jointly enter the ventral lateral nucleus of the thalamus. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022254)]
   :name: ventral thalamic fasciculus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0022254#ventral-thalamic-fasciculus
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022254

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

ventralThalamus
---------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/ventralThalamus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a regional part of brain. Is part of the dorsal plus ventral thalamus. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001900) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Anterior part of the diencephalon that lies between the dorsal thalamus, hypothalamus, and tegmentum of the mesencephalon, including subthalamic nucleus, zona incerta, the fields of Forel, and the nucleus of ansa lenticularis. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001900)]
   :name: ventral thalamus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001900#subthalamus
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001900

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

ventralTrigeminalTract
----------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/ventralTrigeminalTract
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a trigeminothalamic tract and tract of brain. Is part of the pontine tegmentum. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002549) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The Anterior trigeminothalamic tract (or ventral trigeminothalamic tract) serves as pain, temperature, and light touch pathway from the face, head and neck. It receives input from trigeminal nerve, facial nerve, glossopharyngeal nerve and vagus nerve. It receives discriminative tactile and pressure input from the contralateral principal sensory nucleus of CN V, which terminates in the ventral posteromedial nucleus of the thalamus. It then ascends to the contralateral sensory cortex via three neurons. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002549)]
   :name: ventral trigeminal tract
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002549#ventral-trigeminal-tract-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002549

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

   :description: A layer in the central nervous system that lines system of communicating cavities in the brain and spinal cord. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0005358)]
   :name: ventricle of nervous system
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0005358#ventricle-of-nervous-system

ventricularSystemChoroidalFissure
---------------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/ventricularSystemChoroidalFissure
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is an anatomical entity. Is part of the ventricle of nervous system. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002192) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The narrow cleft along the medial wall of the lateral ventricle along the margins of which the choroid plexus is attached; it lies between the upper surface of the thalamus and lateral edge of the fornix in the central part of the ventricle and between the terminal stria and fimbria hippocampi in the inferior horn. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002192)]
   :name: ventricular system choroidal fissure
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002192#ventricular-system-choroidal-fissure
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002192

------------

ventroCaudalCluster
-------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/ventroCaudalCluster
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a midbrain. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2007003)]
   :description: Early-forming neural cluster of the ventral midbrain. At 16 h, axons of the vcc grow caudally to pioneer the medial longitudinal fasciculus (MLF), which is part of the larger ventral longitudinal tract (VLT). Later, neurons from this cluster project axons rostrally into the tract of the post-optic commissure (TPOC). Ross et al., 1992. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2007003)]
   :name: ventro-caudal cluster
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:2007003#ventro-caudal-cluster
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2007003

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a diencephalic nucleus. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2007002)]
   :description: Early-forming neural cluster located in the rostral diencephalon. At 19 h, axons arising from the vrc grow caudally to pioneer the tract of the postoptic commissure (TPOC). Ross et al., 1992. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2007002)]
   :name: ventro-rostral cluster
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:2007002#ventro-rostral-cluster
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2007002

ventromedialNucleusOfHypothalamus
---------------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/ventromedialNucleusOfHypothalamus
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a hypothalamic nucleus. Is part of the intermediate hypothalamic region. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001935) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The ventromedial nucleus (sometimes referred to as the ventromedial hypothalamus) is a nucleus of the hypothalamus. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001935)]
   :name: ventromedial nucleus of hypothalamus
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001935#ventromedial-nucleus-of-hypothalamus-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001935

   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a paravertebral ganglion. Is part of the cervical ganglion. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000408) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: Any of a group of sympathetic ganglia which form two chains extending from the base of the skull to the coccyx along the sides of the spinal column. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000408)]
   :name: vertebral ganglion
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0000408#vertebral-ganglion

   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure

   :description: An epithelium that is part of a vestibular labyrinth. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0006932)]
   :name: vestibular epithelium
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0006932#vestibular-epithelium
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0006932

------------

vestibularGanglion
------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/vestibularGanglion
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a cranial ganglion, sensory ganglion, ganglion of peripheral nervous system and vestibular organ. Is part of the vestibulocochlear ganglion. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002824) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The ganglion of the vestibular nerve. It contains the cell bodies of the bipolar primary afferent neurons whose peripheral processes form synaptic contact with hair cells of the vestibular sensory end organs. Distributed to the maculae of the utricle and saccule and to the ampullary crests of the semicircular ducts. The vestibular fibers arise in bipolar cells in the vestibular ganglion in the internal acoustic meatus. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002824)]
   :name: vestibular ganglion
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002824#vestibular-ganglion-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002824

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

vestibularNerve
---------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/vestibularNerve
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nerve of head region. Is part of the vestibulocochlear nerve. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0003723) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The vestibular nerve is one of the two branches of the Vestibulocochlear nerve (the cochlear nerve being the other). It goes to the semicircular canals via the vestibular ganglion. It receives positional information. Axons of the vestibular nerve synapse in the vestibular nucleus on the lateral floor and wall of the fourth ventricle in the pons and medulla. It arises from bipolar cells in the vestibular ganglion, ganglion of Scarpa, which is situated in the upper part of the outer end of the internal auditory meatus. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0003723)]
   :name: vestibular nerve
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0003723#vestibular-nerve
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0003723

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

   :description: Nuclear complex containing the vestibular nuclei. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002673)]
   :name: vestibular nuclear complex

   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002673

vestibulocerebellarTract
------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/vestibulocerebellarTract
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a tract of brain. Is part of the pontine tegmentum. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002552) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: White matter tract containing axons arising in the vestibular ganglion primarily projecting to the ipsilateral cerebellum via the inferior cerebellar peduncle (Heimer, L. The Human Brain, 2nd ed., 1995, pg 370). [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002552)]
   :name: vestibulocerebellar tract
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002552#vestibulocerebellar-tract-1
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002552

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

vestibulocerebellum
-------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/vestibulocerebellum
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a regional part of brain. Is part of the cerebellum. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014642) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A phylogenetic subdivision of the cerebellum, the oldest part, which regulates balance and eye movements. It receives vestibular input from both the semicircular canals and from the vestibular nuclei, and sends fibres back to the medial and lateral vestibular nuclei. It also receives visual input from the superior colliculi and from the visual cortex (the latter via the pontine nuclei, forming a cortico-ponto-cerebellar pathway). Lesions of the vestibulocerebellum cause disturbances of balance and gait. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014642)]
   :name: vestibulocerebellum
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0014642#vestibulocerebellum
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014642

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>

   :definition: Is a cranial ganglion. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002827)]
   :description: The group of neuron cell bodies associated with the eighth cranial nerve during embryogenesis; splits in later development to form the cochlear and vestibular ganglia. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002827)]
   :name: vestibulocochlear ganglion
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002827#auditory-ganglion
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002827

--------------------

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>

   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a tract of brain. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002768)]
   :description: A long process of a CNS neuron, that carries efferent (outgoing) action potentials from the cell body in the vestibular nucleus of the pons towards target cells in the spinal cord. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002768)]
   :name: vestibulospinal tract
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0002768#vestibulospinal-tract-1

   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/vomeronasalNerve

   :definition: Is a cranial nerve. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0009121)]
   :description: Nerve carrying fibers from the vomeronasal organ epithelium to the accessory olfactory bulb. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0009121)]
   :name: vomeronasal nerve
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0009121#vomeronasal-nerve
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0009121

   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/whiteMatterOfTelencephalon
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a white matter of forebrain. Is part of the telencephalon. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011299) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A portion of white matter that is part of a telencephalon. This can be further subdivided in some species, for example, into hemisphere white matter and the corpus callosum. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011299)]
   :name: white matter of telencephalon
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0011299#predominantly-white-regional-part-of-telencephalon
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011299

whiteMatterOfTheCerebellarCortex
--------------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>

   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a white matter of metencephalon. Is part of the cerebellar cortex. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0024045) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: White matter that lies deep to the granular cell layer of the cerebellar cortex. It contains afferents to the cerebellar cortex and axons that run between the cerebellar cortex and the deep cerebellar nuclei. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0024045)]

   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0024045#white-matter-of-the-cerebellar-cortex-1

------------

zonaIncerta
-----------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/zonaIncerta
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nucleus of ventral thalamus. [auto-generated from 'is_a' property of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001907)]
   :description: The zona incerta is a horizontally elongated region of gray matter cells in the subthalamus below the thalamus. Its connections project extensively over the brain from the cerebral cortex down into the spinal cord. Its function is unknown though several have been proposed related to 'limbic-motor integration' such as controlling visceral activity and pain; gating sensory input and synchronizing cortical and subcortical brain rhythms. Its dysfunction may play a role in central pain syndrome. It is also been identified as a promising deep brain stimulation therapy target for treating Parkinsons Disease. Its existence was first described by Auguste Forel in 1877 as a 'region of which nothing certain can be said'. A hundred and thirty years later in 2007, Nadia Urbain and Martin Deschênes of Université Laval noted that the 'zona incerta is among the least studied regions of the brain; its name does not even appear in the index of many textbooks. '. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001907)]
   :name: zona incerta
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0001907#zona-incerta
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001907

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_

------------

zonaLimitansIntrathalamica
--------------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/zonaLimitansIntrathalamica
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is an anatomical entity. Is part of the dorsal plus ventral thalamus. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0009848) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: A narrow stripe of cells that lies between the prospective dorsal and ventral thalami. This boundary contains signals that pattern the prethalamic and thalamic territories of the future mid-diencephalon. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0009848)]
   :name: zona limitans intrathalamica
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0009848#zona-limitans-intrathalamica
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0009848

------------

zygomaticotemporalNerve
-----------------------

.. admonition:: metadata sheet

   :@context: @vocab: <https://openminds.om-i.org/props/>
   :@id: https://openminds.om-i.org/instances/nervousSystemStructure/zygomaticotemporalNerve
   :@type: https://openminds.om-i.org/types/NervousSystemStructure
   :definition: Is a nerve of head region. Is part of the maxillary nerve. [auto-generated from properties of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0036264) ('is_a' and 'relationship')]
   :description: The zygomaticotemporal nerve or zygomaticotemporal branch (temporal branch) is derived from the maxillary branch of the trigeminal nerve (Cranial nerve V). It runs along the lateral wall of the orbit in a groove in the zygomatic bone, receives a branch of communication from the lacrimal, and passes through zygomaticotemporal foramen in the zygomatic bone to enter the temporal fossa. It ascends between the bone, and substance of the Temporalis muscle, pierces the temporal fascia about 2.5 cm. above the zygomatic arch, and is distributed to the skin of the side of the forehead, and communicates with the facial nerve and with the auriculotemporal branch of the mandibular nerve. As it pierces the temporal fascia, it gives off a slender twig, which runs between the two layers of the fascia to the lateral angle of the orbit. [definition of the [UBERON ontology term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0036264)]
   :name: zygomaticotemporal nerve
   :preferredCrossReference: https://knowledge-space.org/wiki/UBERON:0036264#zygomaticotemporal-nerve
   :preferredOntologyIdentifier: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0036264

`BACK TO TOP <Terminologies: NervousSystemStructure library_>`_
